title: luzula multiflora subsp. congesta
intro: Luzula multiflora subsp. congesta, also known as congested woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of congested woodrush include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, congested woodrush is often used as a tea or tincture, and its roots and leaves can also be dried and used in infusions or added to salves and ointments for topical application.
